blocks.content: He was already at the fountain when she arrived.
Hands in his pockets. Eyes somewhere in the middle distance, the way he had of looking at things without quite landing on them, like he was searching for the frame.
Cassandra slowed a step before he saw her.
She wanted just a second of it.
Just him, standing at the edge of the stone path, the morning light catching the planes of his face, Gerald almost certainly still at home on his desk—
She had guessed right about that.
The thing he did with his expression when he tried not to look relieved—she was learning to recognize it. The slight settling. The half-second where his whole face just—
She laughed and came to stand beside him.
For a moment they both looked at the fountain.
Still sputtering. Still uneven. Still trying.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: I thought about coming here last night.
Neither of them made anything of it.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: You're the local artist. I want the tour.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: I want the Ethan Carter version of this city.
She turned to face him fully.
He was quiet for a moment.
Really think—not the performative pause people did when they wanted to seem considered. Actually going somewhere, turning something over.

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: There's a gallery. Small. Most people walk past it.
